commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4ceea9f7df9f2b9b9a049e712bf6e09655e0c869)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens plugin/runtime schema and metadata readers across Codex app-server, agent tools, gateway/plugin startup, provider routing, message actions, and shared JSON/string helpers against unreadable or non-JSON-safe synthetic data.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection and the PR body give a high-confidence synthetic reproduction class for unreadable plugin/provider/schema metadata; I did not run the tests in this read-only review. The specific review finding is source-reproducible by giving config.secrets.providers[ref.provider].source a throwing getter during manifest config-signal evaluation.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This is a draft, protected maintainer PR with a concrete review finding plus broad compatibility/auth-provider/availability risk that needs owner review before merge.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened runtime/plugin schema and metadata boundaries so malformed or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, diagnosed, audited, or treated as absent instead of crashing assistant startup, doctor/setup flows, provider selection, channel authorization, Codex app-server routing, gateway startup planning, native approval routing, SDK helper APIs, realtime voice consult tool selection, MCP plugin-tool serving, embedded-agent tool-name allowlist collection, message-provider tool filtering, deferred follow-up tool description rewriting, message action validation, tool policy filtering/audit, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent schema diagnostics, plugin runtime tool resolution, central runtime schema projection, embedded system-prompt rendering, system-prompt report construction, or outbound message action handling.
- Kept tests synthetic: current fixtures use names like `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, and `fuzz_move_*`, not the reported third-party plugin name.
- Latest slices guard malformed explicit manifest/capability `authSignals`, Codex app-server command approval decision allowlists, local-model lean tool filtering, embedded-agent tool-name allowlist collection, deferred follow-up tool description rewriting, message-provider tool filtering, realtime voice consult custom tools, MCP plugin-tool descriptor handling, embedded system-prompt construction, tool policy filtering/audit, system-prompt report construction, effective tool inventory, central runtime schema projection, plugin factory-returned tool descriptors, provider tool descriptors, embedded-agent provider tool diagnostics, outbound message actions, native approval routing, provider wizard metadata, manifest/channel metadata, Codex image payload handling, and embedded-agent schema quarantine.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval/outbound-action metadata can crash or widen decisions before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, approval forwarding, manifest/capability auth gating, local-model lean filtering, realtime voice consult tool selection, MCP plugin-tool serving, message-provider policy filtering, embedded-agent tool-name allowlist collection, deferred follow-up tool description rewriting, message action validation, tool policy filtering/audit, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent schema diagnostics, plugin runtime tool resolution, central runtime schema projection, embedded system-prompt rendering, system-prompt report construction, or Codex app-server handling complete.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; broad remote validation on direct AWS Crabbox Linux runners because Blacksmith Testbox auth was unavailable in this shell.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for embedded-agent tool-name allowlist collection with unreadable synthetic local and client tool names while preserving healthy `read`, `exec`, and `mockplugin_client` siblings.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for local-model lean tool filtering with an unreadable synthetic tool name while preserving healthy `read` and `exec` siblings.
- Direct focused Vitest repro for Codex command approval decision allowlists that become unreadable; unreadable allowlists now decline instead of approving.
- Direct focused Vitest repros for malformed explicit manifest/capability `authSignals`; malformed explicit signals now fail closed instead of falling back to aliases/default provider auth.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for deferred follow-up tool description rewriting with an unreadable synthetic tool name while preserving healthy `exec`, `process`, and `cron` siblings.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for message-provider tool filtering with an unreadable synthetic tool name while preserving a healthy `read` sibling and dropping `tts` for voice providers.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for realtime voice consult tool selection with unreadable synthetic custom tool descriptor fields (`name`, `parameters`) while preserving builtin `openclaw_agent_consult` and a healthy `mockplugin_lookup` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for MCP plugin-tool serving with unreadable synthetic tool descriptor fields (`name`, `parameters`) while preserving a healthy `mockplugin_store` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for embedded system-prompt construction with an unreadable synthetic tool descriptor `name` while preserving a healthy `sessions_spawn` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for tool policy filtering and audit with unreadable synthetic core/plugin tool names while preserving a healthy `exec` sibling and auditing malformed drops as `tool[index]`.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for system-prompt report construction, effective inventory, central runtime schema projection, plugin factory-returned tools, provider tools, embedded-agent diagnostics, message action threading/writes/normalization, provider wizard metadata, manifest channel metadata, native approval approver iterators, shared action params, structured attachments, and JSON action params.
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tool-name-allowlist-final-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-name-allowlist.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-capability-provider-runtime-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/capability-provider-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-deferred-followup-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.deferred-followup-guidance.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-message-provider-policy-final-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-realtime-consult-tools-full node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/talk/agent-consult-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-plugin-tools-mcp-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-embedded-system-prompt-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/system-prompt.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tool-policy-pipeline-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/agents/tool-policy.plugin-only-allowlist.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-system-prompt-report-post-rebase-2dcc node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/system-prompt-report.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tools-effective-inventory-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tool-schema-projection-c009 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-active-tool-schema-warnings-c009 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.threading.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-final-pr-update-after-latest-main node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/memory-embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-config-owner.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-tool-availability.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.media-factory-plan.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` and `node_modules/.bin/oxlint` on the latest touched file sets.
- `git diff --check`
- Added-line scrub for reported plugin names, local paths, and private network literals.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Evidence after fix:

- Latest embedded-agent tool-name allowlist slice proof after rebase to `9596b7bd7a`: `tool-name-allowlist.test.ts` passed 1 file / 9 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, local autoreview after fixture type cleanup, and branch autoreview after the capability-provider rebase conflict all passed with no accepted/actionable findings. The conflict-touched `capability-provider-runtime.test.ts` also passed 1 file / 47 tests.
- Latest deferred follow-up description slice proof after rebase to `6f9d5e1b95`: `agent-tools.deferred-followup-guidance.test.ts` passed 1 file / 3 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Latest message-provider policy slice proof after rebase to `5a6472718d`: `agent-tools.message-provider-policy.test.ts` passed 1 file / 8 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed. Branch autoreview during the PR update reported no accepted/actionable findings.
- Latest realtime voice consult slice proof after rebase to `01d9963e4e`: `agent-consult-runtime.test.ts` passed 1 file / 8 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed with no accepted/actionable findings.
- MCP plugin-tools bridge slice proof after rebase to `c237de552a`: `plugin-tools-serve.test.ts` passed 1 file / 8 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Embedded system-prompt slice proof after rebase to `c8cc010e09`: `system-prompt.test.ts` passed 2 Vitest projects / 18 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Tool policy descriptor slice proof after rebase to `47e86bc1ac`: `tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts`, `tool-policy.test.ts`, and `tool-policy.plugin-only-allowlist.test.ts` passed 4 Vitest projects / 67 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed after addressing the audit under-count finding.
- Local-model lean, Codex approval, and malformed auth-signal slices were rebased to `b43910b590`; the final node-wrapper focused suite passed 10 files / 146 tests, and the final clean rebase had no path overlap with the latest gateway image-budget main change.
- Branch autoreview completed clean with no accepted/actionable findings after the malformed auth-signal follow-up; local autoreview also completed clean on the approval and auth-signal fixes.
- System-prompt report, effective inventory, central schema projection, plugin runtime tool descriptor, embedded-agent provider diagnostics, provider-tool descriptor, and message threading slices have focused Vitest proof plus oxfmt/oxlint/diff/scrub proof recorded in this PR.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate after the provider-wizard slice: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_991a8c29acdf`, run `run_41811346ac17`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 on base `8e806e9125`.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate for the branch: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_52b1fe398bd3`, run `run_c85914033aaa`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 over core, coreTests, extensions, and extensionTests.
- Current branch head after push: `4ceea9f7df9f2b9b9a049e712bf6e09655e0c869`.
- Latest rebase base: `b43910b59089cfea1eb0a59ebb7948de2120c7d7`.
- Latest full focused proof base: `6fdf6b0680e47dcab2316b258f6bc3213a05d5fa`; after rebasing over the non-overlapping gateway image-budget commit, `git diff --check` and scrub passed again.
- Current added-line scrub: no reported plugin name, local path, or private network literal in the branch diff.

Observed result after fix:

- Hostile synthetic config/schema/provider/manifest/native-approval/message-action/plugin-tool inputs are bounded at the relevant owner boundary instead of throwing raw getter/enumeration/serialization/spread errors.
- Embedded-agent tool-name allowlist collection omits unreadable malformed local/client tool names while preserving healthy readable siblings and registered/core allowlist entries.
- Deferred follow-up description rewriting treats unreadable malformed tool names as opaque non-matches while preserving the malformed entry and healthy readable siblings.
- Message-provider tool filtering omits unreadable malformed tool names under provider-specific policies while preserving healthy readable siblings and duplicate counts.
- Realtime voice consult tool selection omits unreadable malformed custom realtime tools while preserving the builtin consult tool and healthy custom siblings.
- MCP plugin-tool serving omits unreadable malformed plugin-tool descriptors before wrapping/listing them, while preserving healthy sibling tools and existing pre-wrapped approval behavior.
- Embedded system-prompt rendering omits unreadable prompt-only tool names while preserving healthy sibling tool names.
- Central runtime schema projection reports unreadable tool descriptor fields as diagnostics and filters only affected tools, preserving healthy siblings.
- Effective tool inventory uses readable tool names for lookup and lets schema projection quarantine unreadable descriptors while preserving healthy sibling inventory entries.
- Tool policy filtering and plugin group expansion drop unreadable-name descriptors without raw-crashing, and audit logs include `tool[index]` placeholders so malformed drops remain counted.
- Local-model lean filtering omits unreadable malformed names while lean filtering is active and still preserves the original tool array when lean mode is inactive.
- Codex command approval decision allowlists now fail closed when unreadable instead of granting session or one-shot approval.
- Explicit manifest/capability `authSignals` are authoritative: malformed/unreadable/empty signal lists do not fall back to aliases or canonical provider auth.
- System-prompt report construction treats unreadable tool descriptor fields as absent, uses stable `tool[index]` fallback names for unreadable names, and still reports healthy sibling tools.
- Provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, provider diagnostics, plugin runtime tool resolution, message action normalization/threading/writes, provider wizard setup choices, gateway startup manifest planning, native approval routing, manifest tool availability, Codex image payload handling, and embedded-agent schema-quarantine fixtures retain their previously proven behavior.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because `blacksmith auth login` is required in this shell; direct AWS Crabbox was used for broad remote gates.
- The latest broad Crabbox gate predates the newest outbound runner/normalization/threading/provider-tool/runtime-diagnostics/plugin-tool-descriptor/projection-descriptor/effective-inventory/system-prompt-report/tool-policy/embedded-system-prompt/MCP-plugin-tool/realtime-voice-tool/message-provider-policy/deferred-followup/tool-name-allowlist/local-lean/approval/auth-signal slices; those slices have focused local proof and autoreview/static proof as listed above.
- The newest `main` commit after the 10-file focused proof touched only gateway image-budget files with no branch-path overlap; the branch was rebased over it and passed `git diff --check` plus scrub, but the 10-file focused Vitest suite was not rerun after that final no-overlap rebase.
- After the push, target `main` advanced again to `1e5ccd1ce82e91d25369815479b82aa96e013c9a` in Matrix/number-coercion files with no branch-path overlap; no additional rebase has been pushed for that non-overlap drift.
- GitHub PR checks are queued/running on the pushed head.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No new config surface. Existing malformed/unreadable config, manifest, provider, tool descriptor, diagnostic, or action inputs now fail closed or resolve as absent at bounded reads.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed or unreadable tool/provider/plugin/native-approval/action metadata is rejected, omitted, or quarantined earlier instead of crashing runtime paths or broadening access.

What is the highest-risk area?

Overly strict sanitation could omit a valid provider tool descriptor, plugin tool descriptor, MCP plugin-tool descriptor, realtime custom voice tool, diagnostic payload, runtime projection descriptor, prompt-only tool name, message-provider-visible tool, deferred-followup-visible tool, embedded-agent allowlist-visible tool, or outbound action parameter.

How is that risk mitigated?

The changes preserve readable sibling entries, fail closed only for route/control/identity/idempotency-style action params in the newest outbound slices, keep unreadable payload-only fields as absent, preserve numeric alias behavior, preserve MCP pre-wrapped approval behavior, preserve duplicate readable tool counts in message-provider filtering, keep deferred follow-up unreadable names as non-matching opaque entries, keep unreadable allowlist names omitted without losing healthy siblings, and use focused regressions plus broad AWS `check:changed` gates from earlier branch slices.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the draft PR at head `4ceea9f7df9f2b9b9a049e712bf6e09655e0c869`, or continue the next fuzzing slice from the current `main` tip and rebase over the latest non-overlap Matrix/number-coercion change.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI is queued/running after the force-push. No local actionable autoreview findings remain. The pushed branch is rebased onto `b43910b59089cfea1eb0a59ebb7948de2120c7d7`; target `main` later advanced to `1e5ccd1ce82e91d25369815479b82aa96e013c9a` with no branch-path overlap.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local/branch autoreview findings were addressed, including Codex URL-backed image-block preservation, provider wizard receiver handling, native approval false-positive target matching, shared param-reader silent truncation, message action `topLevel`/`idempotencyKey` fail-closed handling, numeric legacy-target preservation, guarded message action threading controls, guarded message action threading writes, guarded provider tool descriptors, guarded embedded-agent provider diagnostics, guarded plugin runtime tool descriptors, guarded central schema projection descriptors, guarded effective inventory name lookup, guarded system-prompt report descriptor reads, accurate audit counting for malformed tool-policy drops, guarded embedded system-prompt tool names, guarded MCP plugin-tool descriptors, guarded realtime voice consult tools, guarded message-provider tool names, guarded deferred follow-up tool names, guarded embedded-agent allowlist tool names, fail-closed Codex approval decision allowlists, and fail-closed malformed manifest/capability auth signals.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
